weather_report:
Mid-morning in Subiaco arrives with scattered clouds drifting like torn silk across a bright blue sky, letting generous light pour through. The air looks clean and views feel crisp; it’s a pleasant, open sort of day. Expect sunshine to win more often than not, with cloud streaks offering passing shade. Even with the cloud, the sun will have some bite as the day leans toward midday, so the UV will run strong; it’s the kind of light that rewards sunglasses, a hat, and a touch of sunscreen.

The hours ahead look kindly balanced: mostly bright, dry in feel, and easy to be out in. Light layers will serve you well—something you can unbutton in the sun and keep on in the shade. It’s a fine moment to walk to the bakery, linger at a small outdoor table, or wander to the shops and let the morning do its gentle work. If you’re staying in, throw open a window, water the plants, or settle with a book in the sunlit corner; if you’re heading out, a short ride or an unhurried errand on foot will pair nicely with the clear light and soft cloud.
